commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Copilot `api.runtime.state` guard and a new metadata-only runtime stub used by the plugin CLI metadata loader.

PR surface: Source +83. Total +83 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main dereferences `api.runtime.state` in the Copilot entrypoint while the CLI metadata loader passes an empty runtime object. I did not run the reporter's installed `openclaw plugins list` or `openclaw status` path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the branch requires contributor real-behavior proof and a maintainer decision on the plugin SDK boundary; automation cannot supply the contributor's installed-runtime proof.

target.title: fix(copilot): guard against undefined runtime.state during cli-metadata registration
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94516 — copilot plugin crashes during gateway startup with:
```
[plugins] copilot failed during register from .../@openclaw/copilot/dist/index.js:
TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'openSyncKeyedStore')
```

**Root cause**: `buildPluginApi()` in `cli-metadata` registration mode passes `runtime: {} as PluginRuntime` (empty object cast to `PluginRuntime`) to the plugin's `register()` callback. Any plugin that dereferences `api.runtime.state` during `register()` hits `undefined` and crashes.

## Why This Change Was Made

The crash path is in `src/plugins/loader.ts:3338-3348`:
- For non-`setup-runtime` registration modes, `buildPluginApi` receives `runtime: {} as PluginRuntime`
- `copilot/index.ts:33` calls `api.runtime.state.openSyncKeyedStore(...)` unconditionally
- The real `runtime.state` object is only available via the Proxy-based lazy runtime resolution in `setup-runtime` mode

The fix adds a guard: if `api.runtime.state` is undefined, silently return — the plugin will be fully registered later when the setup-runtime path activates.

## User Impact

- Gateway starts without copilot plugin crash on startup
- No functionality lost: the guard only skips early registration in cli-metadata mode; the plugin is fully activated via setup-runtime at runtime

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

**Behavior addressed**: Fix copilot plugin crash caused by missing `api.runtime.state` during cli-metadata registration phase.

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: Node v24.13.1
- **Gateway**: N/A (crash reproduced via node script)

**Exact command run to reproduce crash**:
```js
// Simulates loader.ts:3348 passing {} as PluginRuntime
const api = { runtime: {} };
api.runtime.state.openSyncKeyedStore();
// → TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'openSyncKeyedStore')
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```
GUARD: runtime.state is undefined, silently returning (plugin will register via setup-runtime)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: The guard `if (!api.runtime.state) { return; }` prevents the crash. The plugin will be fully activated later when `setup-runtime` provides the real runtime object.

**What was not tested**: Full gateway startup with copilot plugin enabled (pnpm install missing in this worktree for `ipaddr.js` dependency). The fix is a pure guard pattern — no new code paths are introduced.

## Risk checklist

- No config changes, no migration needed
- The guard path is purely additive — existing setup-runtime registration is unaffected
- Affected file: `extensions/copilot/index.ts` (+6 lines)

## Current review state

What is the next action?
- Maintainer review

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?
- CI green (test deps need `pnpm install` in worktree)
- `ui/` tsgo errors are pre-existing, unrelated

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?
- None yet

Closes #94516
target.author: sunlit-deng

commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
